description: Evaluates access management service maturity—access control systems, unified network and application provisioning, context-aware policies (identity, posture, attributes), remote and zero-trust access, separation of duties, access matrices and certification, guest and out-of-band access, data access, non-human identity, AI-era access governance, custodianship, and SOC logging.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: An access management service exists and is reviewed on a regular cadence for efficacy.

capabilities.name: KPIs/KRIs
capabilities.description: Access management KPIs and KRIs—including entitlement hygiene, certification completion, and deprovisioning velocity—are documented and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Mean time to revoke access after role change or termination

capabilities.name: Access Management System
capabilities.description: Centralized access control technology manages enforcement points with dynamic policy deployment, policy-as-code, and policy decision point (PDP) configuration review.
evaluation_focus: Access control for all enforcement points
Centralized vs siloed policy decision points
PDP configuration review aligned to policies
Abstracted policy push to enforcement points

capabilities.name: Unified access provisioning (network and application)
capabilities.description: Network, endpoint, and application need-to-know access is provisioned via formalized processes with automation, consistent scope across locations and systems, and regular review.
evaluation_focus: Formalized network and endpoint need-to-know access process
Consistent access provisioning across locations and systems

capabilities.name: Context-aware access policies
capabilities.description: Access decisions at all policy enforcement points (PEPs) incorporate identity, device posture, and enterprise asset attributes as zero-trust context signals.
evaluation_focus: Identity-based rules at all enforcement points
Device posture in network and remote access
Subject posture considered from all locations
Asset attributes considered for all subjects

capabilities.name: Remote access and zero-trust network access
capabilities.description: Remote access is formalized with consistent policies across gateways, zero-trust network access (ZTNA) or secure service edge (SSE), and identity plus device posture checks.
evaluation_focus: Consistent remote access policy across gateways and LAN
Cloud SSE or ZTNA for internal asset access
Zero-trust remote access with identity and posture checks

capabilities.name: Access Isolation (Separation of duties) and its automation
capabilities.description: Production, development, and deployment access is separated with automated CI/CD removal of development access from production.
evaluation_focus: User separation across production, development, and deployment
Separate permissions for service and normal accounts
Automated CI/CD removal of dev access from production

capabilities.name: Access Matrix and entitlement certification
capabilities.description: A granular subject-to-object access matrix applies enterprise-wide, is reviewed regularly, and is supported by periodic access recertification campaigns.

capabilities.name: Guest Access
capabilities.description: Guest user onboarding is formalized with automated provisioning and deprovisioning.

capabilities.name: Out-of-band (OOB) device access
capabilities.description: Out-of-band (OOB) management network access is formalized with secure protocols, consistent access control across source locations, and regular review.
evaluation_focus: Consistent OOB access control across source locations

capabilities.name: Data access
capabilities.description: Data access is formalized with location-independent access and regular review.

capabilities.name: Access Management Service custodian
capabilities.description: A dedicated access management service manager maintains separation of duties from authentication, credential management, and directory or identity provider administration.

capabilities.name: Access, authorization and accounting and logging
capabilities.description: Access logs, activity, and lifecycle changes are logged securely to the SOC, including dynamic changes and anomalous access policy modifications.
evaluation_focus: Secure access activity logging to SOC

capabilities.name: Non-human and workload identity access
capabilities.description: Service principals, workloads, API keys, OAuth client credentials, and automation or AI agent accounts are governed with lifecycle-bound access policies.
evaluation_focus: Workload and service principal access lifecycle
API key and OAuth client credential governance
Automation and AI agent account access boundaries

capabilities.name: AI-era access governance
capabilities.description: Policies govern approved versus shadow AI tool access to enterprise data and delegated agent actions on behalf of users.
evaluation_focus: Approved versus shadow AI tool access to enterprise data
Policy for delegated agent actions on behalf of users

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
